revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 pixel-art terrain-tile autotile sheet, arranged as 4 columns x 4 rows of equal 16x16px frames, 16 frames total. SNES 16-bit platformer/world-map pixel art, crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, no grid lines, no labels, no tile IDs, no UI, no text, no watermark. Every frame must stay inside its own 16x16 cell and keep the same scale. Opaque pixel art; fill any truly empty space with solid #FF00FF magenta only, but terrain/water should fill each tile naturally.

Subject: top-down Japanese satoyama fantasy RPG river autotile sheet. The river is low terrain H0 and surrounding grassland is H1. World-map material scale: broad geographic terrain, not close-up ground detail. Warm golden-hour light, top-left light source, cheerful palette. Primary water color #5b8fb8 blue-green. Bank shadows #2a3a28. Grass lip #8abf5a. Dark one-pixel outline where useful. Cohesive palette similar to hand-painted 16-bit Japanese countryside tiles.

Row 1, frames 0-3: river center variations. Calm blue-green mountain stream water, gentle north-to-south ripple lines, soft highlights, subtly animated-looking flow, minimal white foam, zero large rocks, readable water that does not overpower.

Row 2, frames 4-7: edge transitions N, S, W, E. Each tile must clearly show height difference: bright grass lip on the higher land, then an eroded cut bank with exposed dark earth, hanging grass roots, small mossy stones, and a dark wet shadow band directly at the water edge. The water surface must read as one tile lower than the land. Avoid hard rectangular bands; make natural irregular shore edges.

Row 3, frames 8-11: outer corner transitions NW, NE, SW, SE. Curved natural river bends, same eroded cut bank, exposed earth, hanging roots, mossy stones, and dark wet shadow. No right-angle corners.

Row 4, frames 12-15: accent variants. Frame 12 gentle foam at a curve. Frame 13 river with sparse pebbles. Frame 14 dark deep-water center. Frame 15 foam near a small rock. Keep rocks small and sparse; no large rocks.

Tilemap quality rules: final sheet must not read as a visible checkerboard. Vary texture, edge breakup, and lighting between cells while keeping set cohesive. Include usable center, edge, corner, chipped/broken shoreline, grass-overlap, shallow water, small wave highlights, and decoration variants. Terrain transitions should blend grass, dark earth bank, shadow, and water naturally, never as straight vertical or horizontal strips of water next to land. Curved land-water boundaries, beaches/shallow wet edge where appropriate.

Avoid all protected or unrelated elements: no Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, likenesses, or emblems. No characters, no creatures, no birds, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no guns, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no isometric perspective, no vanishing point.

Output one coherent 64x64 PNG sheet only, crisp 16-bit-style pixel art, each 16x16 frame cleanly contained with no visible grid borders.
